mnemonics: A Very Clever Man Puts Coffee Pots On Networks (Kevin Ashton, 1999)
keyPoints: The term 'Internet of Things' was coined by Kevin Ashton in 1999 while working on supply chain optimization at Procter & Gamble.
The initial concept was about connecting RFID-enabled objects to the internet for tracking and inventory management.
The genesis of IoT is rooted in the convergence of wireless technologies, micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS), and the internet.
commonMistakes: Believing IoT is a 21st-century concept; its foundational ideas (like a networked appliance) existed as early as 1982.
Attributing the creation of IoT to a large tech company instead of an individual (Kevin Ashton) working on a specific supply chain problem.
